title: The Opening
story_beat: Pink Bear wakes up to a snowy Easter morning, dreaming of the perfect egg hunt, and the reader meets Susie and their cozy, toy-filled home.

techniques.name: character_voice
techniques.reason: Essential for bringing Pink Bear to life and making the reader connect with their unique perspective from the very first page.

techniques.name: sensory_detail
techniques.reason: To quickly remind Chris how to use senses to paint a clear picture of Pink Bear's world and the unexpected snowy Easter morning.

techniques.name: show_dont_tell
techniques.reason: To ensure Pink Bear's personality and the story's initial conflict are revealed through actions and observations, not just stated directly.

wonder_prompt: Think about your favorite storybook character. What makes them feel so real, like you can almost hear them talking or know exactly what they're thinking? How do authors make us feel that deep connection right from the start?
wonder_example: Corduroy was a bear who once lived in the toy department of a big store. Day after day he waited with the other animals for someone to come along and take him home. The store was always filled with shoppers, but no one ever seemed to want a small bear without a button. (Don Freeman, Corduroy)
build_instructions: It's time to bring Pink Bear to life! Imagine Pink Bear waking up on Easter morning inside Susie's cozy colonial New England home. What's the very first thing Pink Bear sees, hears, or feels? Does the light green siding outside filter the morning sun differently? Show us the scattered toys on the floor, maybe a glimpse of Ruby, the elderly Patterdale terrier. Most importantly, capture Pink Bear's unique voice and excitement for the Easter Egg Hunt, even as the first hint of snow outside the big, bright windows threatens to complicate everything. Let the reader feel Pink Bear's anticipation and the warmth of this special home on pages 1-3.
reflect_preview: Next time, we'll explore how Pink Bear reacts to the unexpected snow. Will the perfect Easter Egg Hunt still be possible, or will this snowy surprise lead to an even bigger adventure for Pink Bear and Susie?
